valencian-rice-teleculinaria.name: Valencian Rice
instructions: Take to the fire a big pot with the margarine and the olive oil. Add the squid rings and let it skip. Add the tomato clean and cut into cubes and let it cook until soft. Then add the carrots cut into slices and the green pepper cut into strips. Cover the pot and let it cook a little.

Then add the cut pork into cubes and let it saute a little. Then add the clams and wrap everything. Cook the gambas in salted water for 2 minutes. Drain and reserve the broth after strain. Peel the gambas, reserving some with peel for decoration.

Add the rice to the pot, add the gamba broth and add more water, in which a small portion of saffron has previously diluted, in order to obtain a little more than twice the amount of rice. Let it boil, season with salt and pepper and let it cook for 13 minutes.

Um pouco antes de terminar a cozedura, acrescente as salsichas cortadas em rodelas, as ervilhas e as gambas descascadas. Retire do lume, decore com o pimento vermelho cortado em tiras e as gambas com casca que reservou e sirva de imediato.

veal-with-plums-argiro.name: Veal With Plums

ingredients.item: 11⁄2 kgmoshari queen or spass nodes (full piece)
1 branch of fresh thyme or rosemary
11⁄2 kgmoshari queen or spass nodes (full piece)
1 branch of fresh thyme or rosemary

instructions: We choose beef from the spade, ideal royalist or young calf minds. Leave the meat for 20 minutes at room temperature, covered.
Tie the beef with baking twine to keep its shape, sprinkle with some olive oil, salt and pepper and spread well everywhere.
Heat the olive oil in a pot or cooker and add the meat around for 6–8, until crust is formed. We take the beef out on a platter.
In the same pot, sauté the coarsely chopped vegetarians for 3-4 minutes over high heat.
Add balsamic vinegar and allow 2-3 minutes to evaporate.
Add the herbs, line the orange and leave the piece of fruit in the sauce. Add the wine and let it boil for 2 minutes. Finally add the broth and put the meat back in the pot.
Seal the vessel and cook on low heat for 1 hour until tender. After baking, remove the beef and vegetables from the sauce.
Add plums and chestnuts to the sauce and boil on high heat for 2 minutes until the sauce is glazed and thickened. Remove the string from the meat and cut into thin slices.
Serve the beef with the sauce, accompanied by mashed potatoes and peas.

vegan-blueberry-crisp.name: Vegan Blueberry Crisp
instructions: Preheat the oven to 177°C (175 degrees C).
Place blueberries into an 8-inch square baking dish.
Stir together flour, white sugar, brown sugar, and cinnamon in a medium bowl. Stir in vegan mayonnaise until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs. Sprinkle the mixture over the top of the berries.
Bake in the preheated oven until the filling is bubbly and the top is lightly browned, 35 to 40 minutes.

vegan-chocolate-dessert-argiro.name: Vegan Chocolate Dessert

ingredients.item: 1/2 cup honey (or petimese or other sweetening matter)
1/2 cup honey (or petimese or other sweetening matter)

instructions: For vegan chocolate dessert, you can use black beans boiled, or mix boiled legumes, such as beans, chickpeas etc.
Add the beans, oats, cocoa, honey, dates, oil, baking, zest and 2 pinches of salt to the mixer.
We ground all materials very well to be mashed and made like paste.
Finally, add the chocolate drops and with spatula mix to enter the mixture. (Keep a few drops of chocolate for the sweet surface).
Place non-stick paper in a small baking pan 24 cm or in a small buttered form with detachable base.
Empty the sweet, straighten the surface with spatula. Sprinkle the remaining chocolate drops.
Bake in a well preheated ocean, 160 °C in the air, in the medium grill, 15′ until stabilized.
Let the chocolate cake stand and cool and then refrigerate to cool.
Cut into small bites and serve.

vegan-marshmallows.name: Vegan Marshmallows
instructions: In a large bowl, using a whisk, gently combine powdered sugar and cornstarch.

Grease an 8x8-inch square baking pan with a small amount of oil and then lightly sift about half the powdered sugar and cornstarch mix all over the bottom and sides of the pan. Set aside. Reserve the remaining powdered sugar-cornstarch mix for dusting the cut marshmallows later on.

Pour the aquafaba into the bowl of a stand mixer (or deep mixing bowl if using a hand mixer) fitted with the whisk attachment. Begin to whip the aquafaba on low until it looks foamy or frothy, about 20 seconds.  Then increase the speed to medium-high until stiff peaks are achieved, 10 to 12 minutes (it might take longer depending on temperature, age of aquafaba, and other factors in the kitchen). Once stiff peaks are reached (which means that your whipped aquafaba should stand straight up off the beater without flopping over), turn off the mixer.

First measure out ingredients for syrup. In a medium saucepan add the granulated sugar, corn syrup, and 1/4 cup of the water. Whisk together until it looks like wet sand. Using a pastry brush dipped in water, gently sweep any sugar crystals back into the pan (this will help keep the syrup from crystalizing). Attach a candy thermometer to the side of the pan, careful not to touch the bottom, and place over medium-high heat. Let the mixture come to a boil without stirring. You’ll want the syrup mixture to reach 141°C (soft crack), which may take around 10 minutes (this time can vary a lot—keep a close eye on the thermometer).  As soon as the syrup reaches 141°C, lower your heat as low as it will go and let the mixture sit on low until ready to use.

In a small saucepan, mix the agar-agar and the remaining 3/4 cup water. Turn the heat on medium-low until the mixture begins to simmer, whisking constantly. Cook until small bubbles form around the edges (watch the heat; if the agar-agar starts to boil, it will congeal).  Let it simmer for 3 to 5 minutes, until the mixture thickens slightly and coats the back of a spoon. Remove it from the heat, whisking a final time.

Remove the bowl from the mixer. Using a rubber spatula, gently fold in the agar agar mixture into the whipped aquafaba until just combined, (a few small lumps are okay). You're doing this by hand so the agar mixture does not splash way up on the sides of the bowl the way it would if the mixer were running.

Return the bowl and the whip attachment to the mixer. With the mixer on low speed, slowly pour the hot syrup (watch your hands!) into the bowl.  You may have to turn it on and off a few times if it expands too much. Once all the syrup is in the bowl, turn the mixer on high. It’s normal for the mixture to deflate at this point.  Mix on high until the bottom of the bowl is no longer hot to the touch and the mixture is glossy, about 10 minutes. Towards the end of the 10 minute mark, pour in the vanilla extract and continue to whip.
Pour the slightly cooled marshmallow mixture into the prepared pan, smoothing the top with a rubber spatula.

Allow the marshmallows to sit, uncovered, for 4 to 6 hours, depending on how thick your mixture is, and how dry the environment is. You can test for doneness by nudging the area between the pan and the marshmallow with a spatula or butter knife. If it moves without sticking to the pan, it’s dry.

Sprinkle the top of the marshmallows with the reserved powdered sugar and cornstarch mix (if yours has clumped together, gently stir with a whisk to re-mix). Sift more of the powdered sugar-cornstarch mixture onto a cutting board, then turn the marshmallow from the pan onto the board. Cut to the desired size with a sharp knife, then toss the marshmallows in a bowl with more of the powdered sugar mix.

Transfer to an airtight container and keep at room temperature up to 1 week.  Enjoyed the marshmallows as a sweet snack, use to top your hot chocolate, or make s'mores.      Did you love the recipe? Let us know with a rating and review!

vegan-potato-omelette-with-chickpea-flou.name: Vegan Potato Omelette With Chickpea Flour
vegan-potato-omelette-with-chickpea-flou.original_name: Tortilla de patatas vegana con harina de garbanzos

ingredients.item: 2 tablespoons nutritional yeast (you can buy them here)

instructions: Pelar y cortar las patatas en rodajas finas y la cebolla en tiras finas. Picar los ajos.
Heat some olive oil into a frying pan and add potatoes, onion and garlic. Cooking with medium heat for about 15 minutes or till the potatoes are tender.
In a bowl, mix chickenpox, nutritional yeast, water, salt and pepper up to a homogenous mix.
Add potatoes, onion and garlic to chickenpox and remove well.
Warm up some olive oil in a frying pan and add a blend of chickpeas and potatoes. Cooking with medium heat for about 10-15 minutes or till the omelette is golden.
Turn the omelette around with a plate and cook the other side for a few more minutes. Serve hot or cold.

vegan-red-lentil-stew.name: Vegan Red Lentil Stew
instructions: Place 4 cups water in a pot and bring to a boil. Add peas, reduce heat to low, and cook, 3 to 5 minutes. Remove peas with a slotted spoon. Reserve broth in the pot.
Add lentils to the broth; bring to a boil. Cook until tender, 15 to 20 minutes.
Meanwhile, heat a frying pan over medium-high heat. Add coconut oil to melt. Add potatoes, carrots, and bell pepper, and saute until soft, 7 to 10 minutes.
Once lentils have finished cooking, add the potato mixture, cooked peas, Swiss chard, garlic, salt, nutmeg, and pepper to the pot. Squeeze in lemon juice and serve.

vegetable-pillows-in-phyllo-dough-telecu.name: Vegetable Pillows In Phyllo Dough
vegetable-pillows-in-phyllo-dough-telecu.original_name: Almofadas de legumes em massa filo
instructions: Peel the onion and cut it into half-moons; peel the carrot and curgete and grate them; discard the seeds to the pepper and cut it into strips; cut the leek into thin slices.
Light a frying pan with the olive oil, add the onion, leek, carrot and curget and let it saute a little. Add the pepper, season with salt and pepper, stir and remove from the fire.

Extend a leaf of phylum, brush it with melted butter and overlay another leaf. Cut them into four pieces and fill them with the previous one. Brush the ends with melted butter and close in pillow form. Repeat the operation until the dough and the filling are finished.
Lay the cushions on a tray lined with vegetable paper and put them in the oven, preheated to 180°C, until golden. Serve warm, preferably. If you like, you can go with soy sauce or bittersweet sauce.

vegetable-souffle-pie-with-phyllo-argiro.name: Vegetable Souffle Pie With Phyllo

ingredients.item: 1 kg of different grasses, washed and well drained
Warm water (as much as it takes)
1 kg of different grasses, washed and well drained
Warm water (as much as it takes)

instructions: First we wash the grass thoroughly and drain them.
Finely chop them and salt them and rub them with their hands to sweat and leave them for 15 minutes.
Then we squeeze them tightly to remove their fluids.
Add the leeks finely chopped, fresh onions, herbs, crushed cheeses and trahanas.
Rub a lot of pepper and mix them well.
We grease a large shallow baking pan for pie.
Spread one leaf and oil it by sprinkle.
Then spread the 2nd leaf slightly drenched.
Beat the eggs with the milk and half the olive oil.
Add half the green-cheese filling and pour a spoonful of egg mixture.
Cover with the 3rd leaf slightly strained and empty the rest of the filling.
Pour with a spoonful of egg mixture and cover with the 4th and 5th sheets, both oiled and slightly strained.
We're turning the edges inside.
Gently pour the pie, pour with the rest of the liquids and sprinkle with some olive oil.
Bake the pie in a well preheated oven, at 180-2003

vegetarian-borscht.name: Vegetarian Borscht
instructions: Combine beets and vinegar in a small skillet over low heat: cook and stir until soft, about 15 minutes.
Meanwhile, heat oil in a large skillet over low heat. Add onion; cook and stir for 2 minutes. Add carrots; cook, stirring occasionally, until soft, about 10 minutes. Set aside.
Bring water to a simmer in a large saucepan. Add lentils and cabbage; cook 10 minutes. Add potatoes; cook 10 minutes more. Stir in beets and onion mixture; season with salt and black pepper.
Add tomato paste; simmer until vegetables are tender, about 10 minutes. Serve soup with sour cream and dill.

vegetarian-fried-feta-rice-by-andie.name: Vegetarian Fried Feta Rice By Andie
instructions: Bring water and brown rice to a boil in a saucepan; cook until rice has softened, 15 to 20 minutes. Drain.
Place a steamer insert into a saucepan and fill with water to just below the bottom of the steamer. Bring water to a boil. Add green beans, cover, and steam until tender, 2 to 6 minutes.

Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Saute green beans and rice in hot oil until lightly crisped, 5 to 10 minutes. Stir in feta cheese; drizzle olive oil over the top and season with black pepper.

vegetarian-kofta-curry.name: Vegetarian Kofta Curry

ingredients.prep: such as IKEA®'s frozen vegetable balls

instructions: Heat olive oil and butter in a large saucepan over medium heat. Add cumin seeds, cardamom pods, whole cloves, cinnamon stick, and bay leaf. Cook until spices start to sputter, about 1 minute. Stir in onion, ginger, and garlic. Cook and stir until onion is softened and just starting to brown, about 5 minutes.

Stir in diced tomatoes, coriander, turmeric, cumin, garam masala, cayenne, and salt. Bring mixture to a gentle simmer; add yogurt and stir well. Add frozen vegetable balls. Pour in hot water to thin sauce slightly to desired consistency. Bring mixture back to a simmer and cook, covered, until sauce is thickened and vegetable balls are heated through, 10 to 15 minutes.

veggie-roast-and-tuna-salad.name: Veggie Roast And Tuna Salad

ingredients.prep: toasted, dressed with extra virgin olive oil and a pinch of salt

instructions: Roast the brussel sprouts and bell pepper with za’atar, salt, olive oil and balsamic vinegar at 204°C for 25-30 minutes or more, depending on how charred you like your veggies.
Once the veggies are roasted, take a salad bowl and mix the sprouts, bell pepper and canned tuna together. Then, mix together dressing ingredients and, once combined, toss salad in this.
Fry a few capers and slivers of small onions and garlic in olive oil and add this on top of the salad.
Finish the salad with a final dash of balsamic vinegar, toasted bread croutons, a sprinkle of fresh parsley, za’atar spice mix and red chilli flakes.

veggie-packed-chickpea-pot-pie.name: Veggie-packed Chickpea Pot Pie
instructions: Bring a large pot of water to boil over high heat and add the potatoes. Cook in gently boiling water for 10-15 minutes, until they are tender and can easily be pierced with a fork.
Drain the potatoes in a colander.
Divide the potatoes into two medium bowls and mash half of them. Set aside.
Heat 2 tablespoons of olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat until the oil is shimmering.
Add the onion and garlic, and sauté until the onion is translucent, about 2 minutes.
Add the celery, corn, broccoli, carrot, mushrooms, chickpeas, parsley, dried thyme, and dried rosemary. Add salt and pepper to taste, and stir.
Add the fresh rosemary and thyme sprigs. Stir and cook over medium heat for about 15 minutes, until the vegetables are tender but still slightly firm.
Remove the rosemary and thyme sprigs.
Sprinkle the flour over the vegetables and mix well.
Pour in half of the vegetable stock and stir.
Add the nutritional yeast and stir well.
Pour in half of the remaining vegetable stock and cook for about 2 minutes while continuing to stir.
Incorporate the mashed potatoes and enough of the remaining stock to reach your desired consistency.
Add the diced potatoes and stir.
Pour the vegetable mixture into a 9-inch (23-cm) baking dish.
Mix 2 tablespoons olive oil and 2 tablespoons soy milk in a small mixing bowl.
Brush the outside edges of the baking dish with the mix using a brush or your fingers.
Preheat the oven to 177°C (180°C).
Place the pie crust dough over the top of the baking dish, pressing the edges to make it stick.
Brush the top of the dough lightly with the oil-soy milk wash.
Make a small X-shaped incision on the top of the crust.
Bake for about 45 minutes or until the pie crust is golden.
Enjoy!

vintage-coffee-cocktail.name: Vintage Coffee Cocktail
instructions: Combine Guinness and malt extract, if using, in a small saucepan; scrape in vanilla seeds and add pod. Bring to a boil, reduce heat, and simmer, swirling occasionally, until reduced by one-third, about 12 minutes; remove vanilla bean and let cool.
Whisk cream and 1 ½ oz. Guinness reduction in a small bowl until slightly thickened.
Stir espresso, whiskey, and molasses in an Irish coffee glass until molasses is dissolved. Top with Guinness whipped cream and nutmeg.
